name: Kidflix Rubric
description: Opinionated, values-based rubric measuring what a story's STRUCTURE endorses and glorifies. Weights must sum to 100. Score the narrative structure only: never penalise a film for mature content, violence, language, MPAA rating, or suitability for children - an R-rated film with an ordinary protagonist who earns everything scores HIGH on the relevant dimensions regardless of who should watch it.

wealth.label: Inherited Specialness & Wealth
wealth.question: Does the protagonist matter because of what they DO, or what they were BORN AS / INTO - and is wealth/status the story's endorsed reward? Self-made ascension scores as low as inherited ascension: penalise the endorsed outcome, not the pursuit of a livelihood.
bands: genuinely ordinary protagonist; the story could happen to anyone (anchors: Coco 36, Inside Out 40, Spirited Away 40, Rocky 40 - broke club fighter, Shawshank Redemption 38 - escape earned over decades)
modest born-into status doing narrative work (Moana 35, chief's daughter)

wealth/status exists but is critiqued as hollow, OR the goal is a livelihood rather than status (Encanto 28; The Princess and the Frog 34 - Tiana marries a prince but he is broke, gets a job, and the reward is her restaurant, NOT status)
born-into greatness, unexamined; no depicted grind (Cars 20)
royal-but-burdened; status as isolation, still the source of specialness (Frozen 16)

status ascension is the endorsed reward, born or climbed (Cinderella 0, Aladdin 5, The Lion King 4, Goodfellas 6, Scarface 4, Wolf of Wall Street 2, Willy Wonka 15 - a poor honest kid whose reward is INHERITING THE FACTORY still lands in this band's range)

agency.label: Protagonist Agency
agency.question: Do they solve their own story, or is rescue assembled around them?
bands: solves their own story through their own choices (Moana, Anna in Frozen)
strong but split, diluted, or destiny-assisted
luck-heavy or shared solving
passive; rescued at their own climax (Sleeping Beauty 0, Snow White 2, The Little Mermaid 4 - Eric kills the villain)

core.label: Emotional Core
core.question: What is the story about UNDER the plot?
bands: deep honest core: grief, identity, belonging, fear-vs-love (Inside Out, Coco, Up, Frozen)
real substance, simpler arc (Cars 15)
standard adventure/friendship beats
core is status, validation, or being chosen (Sleeping Beauty 0)

music.label: Music
music.question: Do songs carry the story?
bands: original songs that carry story (Moana, Frozen, Coco)
strong songs with gaps, or performance-central diegetic music
score only, or licensed soundtrack (Cars 5, Inside Out 5)
actively grating or hollow

inclusion.label: Inclusiveness & Anti-Prejudice
inclusion.question: Distinguish PASSIVE ABSENCE from ACTIVE HARM.
bands: inclusion/anti-prejudice IS the lesson; prejudice-as-plot (Zootopia, The Land Before Time, Shrek, Mulan)
strong inclusion content, secondary to the main arc (Lilo & Stitch 8)
meaningful representation; not the lesson (Moana 7, Soul 7)
neutral: nothing taught, no group harmed (non-human casts, Toy Story 5)
homogeneous world presented as default; passive exclusion; missed opportunity (Cinderella 4, Sleeping Beauty 4)
harmful stereotyping or othering PRESENT in the text (Aladdin 2, The Lion King 2, Scarface 2, The Godfather 2, Wizard of Oz 2)
active degradation or othering AS SPECTACLE (Wolf of Wall Street 0, Peter Pan 0)

romance.label: Romance Framing
romance.question: Is marriage/being-chosen the prize?
bands: absent, or actively subverted (Frozen 6, Brave 6)
present but balanced
marriage/being-chosen is the prize (Little Mermaid 0 - her voice for a man; Cinderella 0 - identified by shoe size)
